topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on pain disorders and their neural correlates, including chronic pain, widespread pain, trigeminal neuralgia, sickle cell disease, and knee osteoarthritis. It emphasizes pain perception, central sensitization, and brain network or morphometric differences underlying human pain.

topics.title: Neural Networks in Eating and Obesity Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain network alterations and cognitive control in obesity and eating-related disorders, including appetite regulation, food choice, and developmental effects. It also includes predictive and structural connectivity approaches used to identify candidate biomarkers and characterize disorder-specific neural architecture.

keywords: the dieter 's dilemma attentional focus on health
homeostatic and hedonic neural correlates of food memory relate
neural correlates of food choice modulate
topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Food Choice
topics.description: This cluster centers on how health, taste, and attentional focus shape food choice and food decision processes. It also includes studies of neural correlates, expertise differences, and related interventions such as prebiotic intervention.

keywords: cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
topics.title: Brain-Body Autonomic and Cardiorespiratory Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on interactions between brain activity, cardiac and respiratory physiology, and interoceptive processing across rest and task conditions. It also includes sleep, cerebrospinal fluid oscillations, and multimodal ECG-fMRI approaches to study brain-autonomic coupling.

keywords: causal mapping of transcranial electrical stimulation and craving reduction in addictive disorder
topics.title: Neuromodulation for Addiction and Craving
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related disorders, craving, and inhibitory control, with emphasis on how brain stimulation and connectivity measures can guide treatment. It includes studies of TMS, transcranial electrical stimulation, tDCS efficacy, and resting-state or EEG biomarkers for personalized neuromodulation.

topics.title: Adolescent substance use and craving neurocircuitry
topics.description: This cluster centers on substance use disorders, craving, and reward-related neural circuitry, especially in adolescents and emerging development. It emphasizes alcohol, cannabis, and opioid-related risk, abstinence, and functional connectivity patterns linked to use and craving.

topics.title: Psilocybin and Cortical Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on psilocybin and psychedelic effects on human brain organization, especially changes in connectivity, entropy, and large-scale cortical dynamics. It also includes dynamic modeling and electrophysiological approaches used to characterize network reorganization and spatiotemporal activity patterns.

keywords: fuzzy connectivity reveal methylphenidate effect on action effect binding networks
topics.title: Dopaminergic Modulation of Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stimulants and dopaminergic mechanisms alter large-scale brain activity, connectivity, and cortical dynamics. It includes effects on action-effect binding networks, memory, and reversal learning across fMRI and EEG studies.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain vascular function, including cerebrovascular reactivity, cerebral blood flow, and perfusion-related MRI measures. It also includes related imaging approaches for assessing baseline physiology, vascular impairment, and blood-brain barrier properties.

topics.title: Longitudinal Brain Development and Connectivity
topics.description: Studies focus on how brain structure and function change over development, with attention to sex differences, alcohol exposure, and cerebellar alterations. The cluster also includes connectivity and clustering analyses used to characterize human and mouse neuroimaging and histological findings.

keywords: functional mri compatible cortical wide field fluorescent
map neural correlates of resting state
mouse rest state functional ultrasound imaging
topics.title: Resting-State Neural Imaging in Awake Mice
topics.description: This cluster centers on resting-state and awake mouse neuroimaging approaches used to map neural correlates, brain network dynamics, and cellular activity. It includes calcium-based and hemodynamic/fMRI-compatible methods for studying cortical and astrocytic signals across behavioral states.

topics.title: Primate Social Brain Development and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how primate social environments and naturalistic social interaction shape brain function, especially in amygdala-prefrontal and temporal systems. It also includes comparative and developmental fMRI work across macaques, marmosets, and humans.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
task base fnirs evidence for shared reading
topics.title: Multimodal fNIRS Brain Activity in Cognitive Tasks
topics.description: This cluster centers on fNIRS and related multimodal neuroimaging approaches used to study cortical and regional brain activity during language, reading, and working memory tasks. It also includes work on integrating fNIRS with EEG/MEG and analyzing hemodynamic or spectral signatures.

keywords: choroid plexus volume mediates the link between insomnia severity and memory impairment
topics.title: Sleep, Glymphatic Function, and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep disruption and insomnia relate to cognitive impairment, with particular attention to glymphatic coupling, choroid plexus changes, and locus coeruleus integrity. The abstracts link sleep characteristics and REM-related disorders to brain structure and function in human studies.

topics.title: Sleep-State Brain Dynamics and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain activity and connectivity change across human sleep, especially NREM and REM stages. It highlights dynamic functional connectivity, EEG-fMRI sleep studies, and sleep-related events such as spindles and K-complexes, with links to glymphatic flow.

topics.title: OCD Treatment Response and Circuitry
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, especially CBT and TMS treatment outcomes in youth and adolescents. It emphasizes frontostriatal and corticostriatal circuitry, compulsivity, and neural predictors of clinical response.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Brain Network Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on mindfulness and meditation practices and their associations with brain network organization, coupling, and functional changes. It also includes resting-state and fMRI-based analyses of neural signatures, including stability and altered connectivity patterns.

keywords: clinical translation of eeg dementia predictors
the importance of independent feature selection
topics.title: EEG Biomarkers for Clinical Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on electroencephalography-based biomarkers and machine learning for predicting diagnosis and individual outcomes, especially in dementia-related studies. It also emphasizes clinical translation, multi-center validation, and the importance of reproducibility and independent feature selection.

topics.title: EEG-fMRI artifact handling and MRI compatibility
topics.description: This cluster centers on techniques for reducing artifacts and interference in simultaneous EEG-fMRI and related MRI environments. It includes quality assurance, denoising, and hardware approaches for improving signal integrity and compatibility.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Fatigue in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue in multiple sclerosis, emphasizing how symptoms relate to brain network organization, iron deposition, and neuropsychological measures. It also includes multimodal and functional imaging approaches used to localize fatigue-related mechanisms and distinguish related concepts such as fatigability and mental effort.

keywords: cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
neurometabolic mechanisms of dynamic musical reward processing
music and emotion in functional connectivity
audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Music Emotion and Reward
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music engages reward, emotion regulation, and sensorimotor systems, with emphasis on dynamic affective transitions and neural encoding. It also includes work on functional connectivity, naturalistic fMRI, and memory or skill retention during musical experience.

topics.title: Aging, cognition, and brain health
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurocognitive aging, including cognitive decline, impairment, and reserve across the adult lifespan. It also links brain structure and longitudinal symptom dynamics with genetic, educational, and other modifiable factors affecting brain health.

topics.title: Cytoarchitectonic Brain Atlases and Parcellation
topics.description: This cluster centers on human brain atlases and cytoarchitectonic mapping, especially efforts to define cortical and regional subdivisions from histological and imaging data. It also includes automated parcellation and atlas-based approaches for comparing brain structure across species and datasets.

keywords: medial prefrontal glutamate and default mode network
topics.title: Multimodal markers of excitation imbalance in psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on 22q11.2 deletion syndrome and related psychosis-spectrum conditions, with emphasis on anterior cingulate and medial prefrontal abnormalities. Studies link glutamate, GABA, cortical excitability, and ACC connectivity to disrupted excitation-inhibition balance and altered default mode network function.

topics.title: Neurofeedback for self-regulation and brain modulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurofeedback approaches for training self-regulation, including real-time fMRI, decoded neurofeedback, and multimodal EEG-fMRI methods. The studies examine how these techniques affect stress regulation, functional connectivity, and activity in regions such as the ventromedial prefrontal cortex and posterior cingulate cortex.

keywords: hierarchical organization of sex differences in functional connectivity during youth
contextual functional connectomics of sex differences in cognition
sex prediction use multimodal structural and functional data
topics.title: Sex Differences in Brain Connectivity and Structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on sex differences in brain structure and functional connectivity across development, aging, and cognition. It also includes multimodal approaches for predicting sex and characterizing hierarchical organization in brain networks.

topics.title: Olfactory Processing and Brain Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on human olfactory research, including smell perception, odor-evoked brain activation, and mechanisms underlying olfactory identification and dysfunction. It also includes fMRI-based approaches and related analytic methods used to study neural representation and biomarkers.

topics.title: Neurometabolic Imaging of Glucose and Sleep
topics.description: This cluster centers on PET and related imaging approaches for mapping glucose metabolism, hyperglycemia, and functional brain responses. It also includes work on glymphatic dynamics and neurometabolic changes during NREM sleep and network integration.

keywords: gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
the impact of bioenergetic challenges on functional brain dynamics
topics.title: Neurometabolic Network Organization in the Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on how metabolic and bioenergetic processes shape brain network organization, including connectomic gradients, cortical metabolic connectivity, and functional-metabolic signatures. It links altered metabolism to integration, disease, and individual differences across human and experimental studies.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal Modulation of Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual and menopausal hormonal changes relate to brain connectivity, structure, and emotion regulation. It also includes hormone-related conditions and interventions such as premenstrual dysphoric disorder, oral contraceptive discontinuation, and menopause hormone therapy effects.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound in Essential Tremor Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor and related patient cohorts, with emphasis on cerebellothalamic networks, Vim targeting, and connectivity fingerprints. It also highlights imaging-based functional mapping and longitudinal analyses linked to durable outcomes and network reorganization.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Connectivity and Response
topics.description: Studies in healthy adults and major depression examine how ketamine alters perception, neural information dynamics, and functional connectome organization. The cluster also addresses dose-dependent pharmacodynamics and markers of therapeutic response.

topics.title: Developmental Brain Changes and Risk Factors
topics.description: Studies of brain development across childhood and adolescence, emphasizing structural and physiological changes linked to prenatal exposure, early life stress, and familial or psychiatric risk. The cluster also includes longitudinal neuroimaging findings on subcortical development, cerebellar volume, and brain metabolism.

topics.title: Adolescent Depression and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on adolescent depressive symptoms, major depression, and suicidal ideation/self-harm, alongside individual differences in risk and clinical subtype. It links these themes to brain structure and connectivity measures, especially amygdala rest state functional connectivity, functional brain connectivity, and morphometric similarity network findings.

keywords: effective connectivity versus functional connectivity across visual cortical areas
effect of epidural spinal stimulation on sensorimotor rhythms
topics.title: Connectivity and Brain Signal Studies in Neuroscience
topics.description: This cluster centers on relationships between effective and functional connectivity, brain-signal measurement, and task-related neural responses across cortical regions. It also includes studies of epidural spinal stimulation, sensorimotor rhythms, and epilepsy-related applications, including pediatric contexts.

keywords: local correlation of brain function in youth onset
long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
topics.title: Exercise Effects on Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how acute and chronic exercise relate to brain function, especially connectivity and network stability measures. It also includes work on sex differences, intensity dependence, and links to insulin action and type 2 diabetes.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain and cognitive development during adolescence, including pubertal timing, interhemispheric connectivity, and structural/functional neurodevelopmental pathways. It also includes influences such as music participation, language development, and genetic or environmental factors.

topics.title: Neurodevelopment, Adversity, and Psychopathology Risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how neurodevelopment in youth and adolescence relates to adversity, resilience, and risk for later psychopathology. It emphasizes brain-behavior associations, childhood adversity, and functional connectivity patterns linked to behavioral and emotional outcomes.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation Networks in Parkinson Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation, especially STN-targeted interventions for Parkinson disease, and how network connectivity and personalized models relate to clinical response. It also includes work on programming, connectomic analysis, and neuropsychiatric outcomes.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
brain network modularity predict training relate improvements
topics.title: Neural Development and Math Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental dyscalculia and related math abilities, especially arithmetic, numerical processing, and training-related gains in children. It also highlights changes in brain network organization and connectivity that may support or constrain math learning.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma-Related Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how glioma and brain tumor surgery alter functional and structural brain networks over time. It emphasizes lesion-related connectivity changes, network remapping, and reorganization patterns linked to tumor location and progression.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms and Modulation of Tinnitus
topics.description: This cluster centers on tinnitus as a phantom sound disorder, emphasizing its neural correlates, subtypes, and factors related to loudness and suppression. It also includes structural and functional brain findings and modulation-related mechanisms across auditory, thalamic, and prefrontal systems.

topics.title: Developmental Functional Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional brain networks develop and reorganize across childhood and adolescence, especially in relation to behavior. It emphasizes naturalistic fMRI and individualized network analyses for studying developmental variation and neural dynamics.

keywords: neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Neural and Computational Bases of Risky Decisions
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural signals and computational models explain decision making under risk, uncertainty, and reward feedback. It includes work on reinforcement learning, exploration-exploitation, choice history, and orbitofrontal updating.

topics.title: Fetal Brain Development and Imaging Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on fetal brain development assessed with MRI, including structural and functional features such as the cortical plate, intermediate zone, and white matter maturation. It also includes studies of altered diffusion and connectivity patterns, especially in hypertensive pregnancies.

keywords: personalized spinal cord dynamic functional connectivity
autonomic network resting state functional connectivity
convolutional neural network base spinal cord volume quantification
topics.title: Spinal Cord Connectivity and Recovery Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on spinal cord and brain network changes across conditions such as degenerative cervical myelopathy, spinal cord injury, fibromyalgia, and stroke. It also highlights prediction of functional recovery using personalized connectivity measures and convolutional neural network-based spinal cord volume quantification.

topics.title: Neuroimaging of Traumatic Brain Injury
topics.description: This cluster focuses on traumatic brain injury and concussion, especially mild and chronic forms, across sports-related and clinical contexts. It emphasizes multimodal neuroimaging and network-level changes in cortical and interhemispheric connectivity, white matter, and default mode alterations.

keywords: improve prediction of post stroke language outcomes
topics.title: Stroke Lesion Disconnection and Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how stroke lesions and structural or functional disconnection relate to motor, language, and cognitive outcomes. It also emphasizes prediction of recovery and clinical deficits across acute and chronic stroke settings.

topics.title: Transcranial Ultrasound Neuromodulation Studies
topics.description: These abstracts focus on transcranial ultrasound stimulation and related neuromodulation approaches in human and fMRI-based studies. The cluster emphasizes MRI-guided delivery, network effects, and early evidence of rapid neuroplasticity across targeted brain circuits.

keywords: the first six years of life
topics.title: Infant Brain Development and Normative MRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on MRI-based characterization of infant and early childhood brain structural development, especially in relation to gestational age, postnatal age, preterm birth, and developmental outcomes. It also highlights normative modeling approaches and cross-population variation in early brain maturation.

topics.title: Epilepsy Network Changes in Pediatric Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy, especially focal and pediatric epilepsy, with emphasis on identifying epileptogenic zones and understanding network-level brain alterations. It also includes multimodal approaches such as EEG, fMRI, and connectome analysis for localization and surgical outcome prediction.

keywords: densely sample single subject task fmri data
topics.title: Fast fMRI of Auditory and Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on fast and silent fMRI approaches for probing auditory, prosody, and language-related brain responses. It also includes work on listening effort, temporal dynamics, and methods for improving localization and measurement of neuronal activity.

topics.title: Developmental White Matter and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how white matter development and microstructural change evolve across infancy, childhood, and adolescence. It also links these developmental brain changes to cognitive and brain-behavior outcomes.

topics.title: Early Brain Network Development in Infants
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain functional organization and connectivity develop from fetal and neonatal stages through infancy and early childhood, with emphasis on preterm birth and maturation-related alterations. It highlights network topology, connectome gradients, and longitudinal developmental trajectories in early life.

topics.title: Autism Connectivity and Developmental Heterogeneity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on atypical brain connectivity and developmental organization in autism across childhood and adolescence. It emphasizes heterogeneity, subgroup differences, and longitudinal patterns linked to symptoms and clinical variation.

keywords: an automatic pipeline for sulcal feature extraction
topics.title: Normative fetal cortical folding atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and atlas construction for fetal and neonatal cortical folding development, with emphasis on normative trajectories and gyrification patterns. It also includes pipeline and surface reconstruction methods used to extract sulcal features and build spatiotemporal cortical surface atlases.

keywords: determinant base optimal coil sequence selection
steerable multi target temporal interference stimulation
topics.title: Optimizing Personalized Brain Stimulation Protocols
topics.description: This cluster centers on optimizing noninvasive brain stimulation protocols, especially personalized TES and TMS montage or coil selection strategies. It also includes emerging temporal interference approaches for steerable, multichannel, and sub-centimeter stimulation.

topics.title: Intracranial Connectivity in Human Brain Circuits
topics.description: This cluster centers on intracranial and stereoelectroencephalography studies of functional and effective connectivity in humans, with frequent application to epilepsy and circuit mapping. It also includes work on sensory, memory, and affective processing across specific brain regions such as the insula and mediodorsal thalamus.

keywords: frequency specific epileptogenic patterns long seeg recording
normative eeg mapping reveal frequency specific epileptogenic patterns across long seeg recording
prolonged somatosensory meg high frequency oscillation
topics.title: Noninvasive epilepsy mapping and network dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on using EEG/MEG, normative mapping, and virtual brain models to identify epileptogenic patterns and support personalized noninvasive diagnosis. It also highlights thalamo-cortical and deep dynamic network mechanisms across refractory, generalized, and single-seizure epilepsy cases.

topics.title: EEG Subtypes and Heterogeneity in ADHD
topics.description: These studies use EEG-based approaches to identify neurophysiological heterogeneity and clinically distinct subgroups in ADHD, often linking subtype profiles to attentional dysfunction and performance. The cluster emphasizes normative modeling, resting-state and multi-state EEG, and transdiagnostic subgrouping across psychiatric conditions.

topics.title: EEG biomarkers in autism spectrum disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurophysiological markers and synchronization patterns in autism spectrum disorder, including altered EEG phase dynamics, reduced alpha power, and EEG complexity. It also links these measures to attentional orienting, sensory symptoms, and social engagement in clinical cohorts.

keywords: variability dependent disruption of structural functional coupling
topics.title: Attention Bias and Brain Variability in Anxiety
topics.description: This cluster centers on anxiety-related disorders and behavior, especially social anxiety disorder, excessive online gaming, and attention bias modification. It also highlights altered brain connectivity and variability in the dorsal attention network and related functional coupling, with links to cognitive function and sex differences.

keywords: neural correlates of state and trait emotion regulation a large scale fmri study
topics.title: Neural Bases of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation, especially cognitive reappraisal, relates to neural activity and amygdala regulation across individuals and disorders. It emphasizes large-scale fMRI and meta-analytic efforts to identify shared neural correlates and possible transdiagnostic signatures.

keywords: tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation and cortical silent period duration
uncertainty aware open source bayesian motor threshold estimation tool
topics.title: TMS Stimulation Parameters and Motor Responses
topics.description: This cluster centers on how TMS parameters such as coil orientation, paired-pulse timing, and stimulation sequence shape motor evoked responses and cortical inhibition. It also includes probabilistic and machine-learning approaches for motor hotspot localization and motor threshold estimation.

keywords: hcp rs fmri pre processing pipeline
topics.title: fMRI Denoising and Reliability in Resting State
topics.description: This cluster centers on resting-state fMRI analysis, especially ICA-based denoising, preprocessing, and distortion correction. It also highlights reliability and stability of functional connectivity measures and related image quality improvements.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Social Cognition
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how the brain supports social cognition across naturalistic and experimental contexts, including empathy, perspective taking, fairness, and social decision making. It also includes developmental and clinical differences in these social processes and their neural signatures.

keywords: hippocampal target pipc stimulation dissociates associative memory formation
topics.title: Causal Brain Stimulation in Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on using stimulation and mapping approaches to probe causal roles of cortical and hippocampal circuits in language, memory, and executive control. It links prefrontal-parietal and motor regions with tasks such as language dominance, speech, task switching, and action planning.

keywords: personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
targeting approach for network level brain stimulation
causal contribution of primary motor cortex
topics.title: Personalized Network Brain Stimulation
topics.description: This cluster focuses on noninvasive neuromodulation approaches for probing and enhancing brain networks, especially frontoparietal and motor circuits. It emphasizes personalized targeting, multimodal imaging, and causal tests of stimulation effects on cognition and behavior.

keywords: cerebellar functional topography and dynamic interactions underlie episodic memory in age
topics.title: Aging, Memory, and Brain Network Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging relates to episodic memory, executive function, and broader cognition through changes in cerebellar and cortical organization. It emphasizes functional gradients, dynamic and directional interactions, and structure-function relationships in healthy and typical aging.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Emotion Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain encodes, differentiates, and predicts emotional states across ambiguous, social, and affective stimuli. It highlights biases, interference, and multivariate neural signatures linked to emotion and individual differences.

keywords: multimodal fmri study of trigger processing
topics.title: Neural Threat and Anxiety Processing
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neural and autonomic responses to fear, threat, and anxiety, with particular attention to fear conditioning, extinction, and trigger-related processing. It also includes social anxiety and misophonia as related affective conditions studied through brain and physiological correlates.

keywords: distributional and centile calibration of dti normative models as training sample sizes increase
bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
topics.title: Normative Modeling and Data Harmonization
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling in neuroscience and methods for harmonizing multi-site neuroimaging data. It emphasizes bias correction, centile calibration, and validation of models for brain structure and DTI across heterogeneous datasets.

keywords: modular python framework for constructing biologically annotate connectomes
topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Tools and Workflows
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, browser-based, and modular neuroimaging tools for visualization, analysis, and reproducible workflows. It spans network neuroscience, atlas-based mapping, and cross-species or multimodal brain imaging applications.

keywords: brain signal variability profiles associate with antidepressant treatment response
brain network controllability predicts response to connectivity guide rtm
functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
topics.title: Neural Predictors of Depression Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on how brain connectivity, signal variability, and related neural features predict response to treatments such as antidepressants, electroconvulsive therapy, and psychotherapy. It also includes studies of rumination, mindfulness-based therapy, and neuromodulation approaches.

keywords: subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
temporal dynamics of brain hemodynamics with accelerated
topics.title: Personalized Neuromodulation for Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on individualized brain stimulation and neuroimaging-guided targeting for major depressive disorder, especially treatment resistant depression. It also includes biomarkers of response such as functional connectivity, brain hemodynamics, and remission after neuromodulatory interventions.

topics.title: Language Lateralization and Brain Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on variability in language lateralization and hemispheric reorganization across development, injury, and clinical conditions. It also highlights associated structural and tract-level differences in language networks, especially the arcuate fasciculus and broader white matter organization.

keywords: brain state dynamics during emotional reflection
topics.title: Depression-related brain connectivity dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and related phenotypes, emphasizing how altered functional connectivity and brain-state dynamics relate to symptoms and reward-circuit dysfunction. It also highlights predictive network-level signatures across resting-state and emotional-reflection contexts.

keywords: federated neuroimaging data management platform for multisite studies
topics.title: Federated Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Reproducibility
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, federated infrastructure for managing and sharing neuroimaging workflows across multisite studies. It emphasizes standards, provenance, containerization, and tool integration to support reproducible large-scale analysis.

topics.title: Open Science and Reproducible Neuroimaging Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on open science efforts aimed at improving reproducibility, reliability, and data sharing in neuroscience. It also emphasizes consortium-based tools and workflows for harmonizing neuroimaging data and mapping scientific collaboration networks.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
motor learning through imitation of fully visible
topics.title: Sensorimotor representations in action and body perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how observation, execution, and imitation shape sensorimotor representations for grasping and hand actions. It also includes body ownership and mirror-therapy paradigms that probe body representation networks and cortical control mechanisms.

keywords: shared age specific structural covariance network alterations
transcriptional profiles of white matter morphological network
topics.title: Depression-Related Brain Network Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural, functional, and transcriptional brain network alterations in depression across developmental and age-related contexts. It includes work on major depressive disorder, adolescent depression, and white matter covariance patterns linked to network topology and connectivity changes.

keywords: neural representation of perceived engagement during action observation
long term dynamics of neural representations of natural
topics.title: Neural Dynamics During Naturalistic Video Experience
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain represents and tracks engagement, action, and temporal structure during naturalistic viewing and behavior. It also highlights long-term dynamics, shared neural coding, and reactivation in neural representations of natural stimuli.

topics.title: Naturalistic Brain Encoding Across Scales
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how naturalistic stimuli and computational models explain brain encoding across human and macaque data. It emphasizes linking stimulus features, temporal dynamics, and prediction-related responses across imaging modalities and scales.

keywords: task guide cross subject neural alignment
topics.title: Neural Alignment in Human Vision
topics.description: This cluster centers on how human fMRI and visual processing relate to neural representations in deep neural networks and other artificial models. It emphasizes representational alignment, encoding, and cross-subject or universal brain modeling for understanding visual and semantic organization.

keywords: genetic basis of human hippocampal organization genome wide analysis of multimodal imaging traits
topics.title: Genetic and Epigenetic Brain Structure Variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetics and epigenetic factors relate to brain anatomy, cortical structure, and hemispheric asymmetry across development. It also includes multimodal imaging and genome-wide analyses linking neuroimaging traits to underlying biological variation.

keywords: brain size links neurodevelopment to neurodegeneration
topics.title: Genetic Architecture of Brain Structure Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on how genetic architecture relates to brain structure, cortical folding, white matter microstructure, and other imaging-derived traits across neuropsychiatric and major psychiatric disorders. It also includes work on genetic correlations, pleiotropy, and brain size trajectories linking neurodevelopment to neurodegeneration.

topics.title: Cortical geometry and laminar mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and mapping cortical surface geometry, folding, and thickness across species and individuals. It also includes laminar fMRI and retinotopic approaches for resolving cortical organization in human visual areas.

keywords: cognitive load modulates phase base eeg brain state dynamics
topics.title: Neural Oscillations in Cognitive Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on how oscillatory and aperiodic EEG dynamics relate to attention, executive control, and working memory. The abstracts emphasize brain signal complexity, alpha-band modulation, and cognitive state changes during task performance.

keywords: paired generation of healthy and disease brain biomarker profiles
the transferability of foundation models to cortical folding
topics.title: Foundation Models for Brain Morphometry
topics.description: This cluster centers on applying foundation models and deep learning to brain morphometry, especially cortical folding, brain shape, and abnormal gyration patterns. It also includes representation learning and generation of healthy versus disease biomarker profiles, with methods for tractography and white matter bundle parametrization.

keywords: microstructural basis of structure function coupling across human resting state networks
topics.title: Structure-Function Coupling in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how structural properties of the brain relate to functional connectivity and coupling across resting and task states. It emphasizes multimodal and microstructural explanations for network organization, decoupling, and directed information flow.

topics.title: Structural Connectivity and White Matter Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping brain structural connectivity and white matter organization across humans and primates. It emphasizes tractography-based connectomes, circuit subdivision, and atlas/tract analysis of association and subcortical pathways.

keywords: evolution of the auditory cortex duplication of heschl 's gyrus in great apes
topics.title: Auditory Cortex Evolution in Great Apes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on comparative and evolutionary studies of cortical organization, especially auditory cortex and Heschl's gyrus, across humans and great apes. It also includes work on sulcal topology, brain surface variation, and white matter mapping in primates.

keywords: map resting state eeg microstate sources
microstates pipeline for source level connectivity and classification in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: EEG Microstates in Brain Dynamics and Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG microstate analysis and related pipeline methods for source-level connectivity, classification, and dynamic brain modeling. The abstracts connect these approaches to altered brain states and disorders such as psychosis, multiple sclerosis, and consciousness-related phenomena.

keywords: lifespan spectral rest state eeg normative model
topics.title: Aperiodic EEG Biomarkers Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on EEG-based measures of brain aging, resting-state electrophysiology, and functional connectivity across the lifespan. It emphasizes aperiodic/rhythmic signal decomposition and brain age modeling in both healthy and heterogeneous populations.

keywords: optimize retinotopic mapping for visual field coverage
topics.title: High-Resolution Retinotopic Mapping of Human Visual Cortex
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping retinotopic organization and visual field representations in human visual cortex, especially V1 and related extrastriate regions. It also highlights high-resolution fMRI, cortical geometry, and submillimeter methods used to improve coverage and characterize fine-scale functional profiles.

topics.title: Multisite fMRI Harmonization and Reproducibility
topics.description: This cluster focuses on improving reproducibility and harmonization in neuroimaging, especially fMRI, across sites and studies. It emphasizes site effect correction, quality assessment, and statistical modeling approaches for estimating effect size and variance.

topics.title: Layer-Specific Neural Mechanisms of Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how high-resolution fMRI and laminar analysis reveal layer-specific and circuit-level mechanisms underlying perception, prediction error, and multisensory decision making. It also includes work on auditory mismatch, alpha entrainment, and neurochemical mechanisms linked to improved perceptual decisions.

topics.title: Hippocampal Dynamics in Memory and Navigation
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal contributions to episodic and associative memory, including consolidation, retrieval success and failure, and representational coding. It also links hippocampal activity to navigation, pattern separation, and the influence of prior knowledge on memory representations.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
topics.title: Visual Mental Imagery and Neural Representation
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine the neural basis of visual mental imagery across the imagery spectrum, including aphantasics and vivid imagers. They focus on early visual cortex organization, cortical depth profiles, and spatiotemporal dynamics of imagined simple and complex content.

keywords: assessment of neural correlates of language processing in multilingual speakers
topics.title: Neural Bases of Multilingual Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how language comprehension, speech, and lexical production are supported in multilingual and bilingual speakers. It emphasizes neural correlates, cortical language circuits, and fMRI-based analyses of language processing and related lateralization.

keywords: meg evidence for distributed encoding of parts of speech
prediction of brain responses reveal emotional effects in sentence processing
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Natural Language Comprehension
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human brain supports comprehension of natural, narrative, and sentence-level language, including prediction, emotional effects, and hierarchical structure. It also highlights distributed encoding and interactions between language and multiple-demand networks in MEG and related brain-response studies.

keywords: fmri base brain function symptom integration
topics.title: Subtype Discovery in Depression Imaging Studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying clinically distinct depression subtypes using neuroimaging, machine learning, and large-scale ENIGMA/biobank datasets. The abstracts emphasize brain-based subtype discovery, predictive modeling, and generalizability across samples and disorders.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging signatures of data driven symptom phenotypes
gradient base disruption of hippocampal cortical organization
independent multiscale progression subtypes of psychosis hippocampus memory symptoms
topics.title: Neuroimaging Subtypes in Psychosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and early psychosis, emphasizing subtype and phenotype discovery through multimodal neuroimaging and connectivity analyses. It also highlights hippocampal, dopaminergic, and structural brain alterations associated with symptom dimensions and disease progression.

keywords: developmental brain network trajectories differentiate resilience
topics.title: Schizophrenia Brain Network Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and psychosis-related changes in brain connectivity, network efficiency, and intrinsic activity across resting-state and developmental contexts. It also highlights biomarkers, resilience, and white matter dynamics that may help distinguish patient subgroups and vulnerability.

topics.title: Inflammation and Brain Changes in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on how inflammatory processes and immune markers relate to depression and related psychiatric disorders, including effects of childhood maltreatment and acute inflammatory challenge. It also covers brain connectivity, gray matter, and connectomic alterations linked to these conditions.

topics.title: Trauma, Connectivity, and Suicide Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on trauma-related psychiatric disorders and their links to suicide risk, with emphasis on PTSD, depression, bipolar disorder, and adverse experiences such as childhood trauma and intimate partner violence. The abstracts also highlight neuroimaging signatures of network dysfunction, connectivity alterations, and structural-functional markers in these conditions.

keywords: post mortem human midbrain t2*w mri
topics.title: High-Resolution MRI Vascular Mapping
topics.description: These studies focus on high-field MRI methods for visualizing and quantifying cerebral and intracortical vasculature, including vessel mapping and 3D reconstruction. The abstracts emphasize post mortem and in vivo imaging at 3T and 7T for detailed vascular characterization.

topics.title: Multimodal Imaging of Human Brain Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on multimodal ex vivo and postmortem brain imaging approaches for resolving microstructure, wiring, and cellular organization. It combines diffusion MRI, PS-OCT, histology, and transcriptomic methods to build 3D atlases and validate mesoscopic anatomy.

keywords: magnetic field correlation imaging quantifies microstructural changes
topics.title: Diffusion MRI of White Matter Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI approaches for probing white matter microstructure, including signal models, high b-value acquisitions, and tract-related contrast enhancement. It also includes work on fiber bundle characterization and microstructural changes in structures such as the corpus callosum.

topics.title: Naturalistic Functional Connectivity and Brain Behavior
topics.description: This cluster centers on how naturalistic stimuli and movie watching are used to study functional connectivity, brain networks, and functional brain activity. A major theme is linking individual variability and connectivity measures to brain behavior mapping and forecasting brain states.

topics.title: Visual Hallucinations in Lewy Body Parkinsonism
topics.description: This cluster centers on visual hallucinations and minor visual hallucinations in Lewy body disease and Parkinson disease, with emphasis on disease progression and patient differences. It also highlights proposed neural mechanism and connectivity or structural alterations involving frontal, thalamo cortical, and brainstem regions.

keywords: the cognitive psychiatric phenotype of parkinson ’s disease through brain network dynamics
topics.title: Parkinson Disease Brain Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on altered brain network dynamics in parkinson disease, including dynamic functional connectivity, default mode network connectivity, and executive network changes. It also highlights cognitive, neuropsychiatric, and gait-related symptoms, with biomarker and machine learning approaches used to characterize disease subtypes and progression.

keywords: biophysical large scale brain circuit models
topics.title: Brain Network Modeling and Connectome Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on computational and model-based analysis of brain dynamics, connectome structure, and effective connectivity across scales. It includes work on fMRI, thalamic and cortico-hippocampal interactions, and reconstructing or validating human structural connectivity.

keywords: rs fmri predict subject level pet suvr
topics.title: Dynamic Brain States in fMRI and PET Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling dynamic functional connectivity and recurring brain states in fMRI, often using hidden Markov approaches and related state-space methods. It also includes work linking rs-fMRI patterns to subject-level PET measures such as amyloid burden and SUVR in prodromal Alzheimer disease.

topics.title: MRI Segmentation and Longitudinal Harmonization
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain MRI segmentation methods and their evaluation across datasets, scanners, and longitudinal designs. It also emphasizes harmonization, stability, and reliability of segmentation and morphometric measures.

keywords: brain foundation models a pilot study
longitudinal multimodal neuroimaging fusion for predicting working memory and attention
topics.title: Brain Foundation Models for Phenotypic Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on foundation-model approaches for neuroimaging, especially fMRI, cortical embeddings, and multimodal fusion. The studies emphasize predicting cognition and clinical outcomes such as working memory, attention, and other phenotypic measures, often benchmarking against classical models.

keywords: deep learning base brain age prediction
convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
inference base brain age gap a distributional measure for quantifying brain aging
topics.title: Brain Age Estimation and Aging Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on estimating brain age from MRI using deep learning and normative modeling approaches. It also examines brain age gaps and regional deviations as biomarkers of aging, disease, and intervention effects.

keywords: atlas guided region wise shapley value
topics.title: Explainable Deep Learning for Brain MRI
topics.description: This cluster focuses on using deep learning and explainable AI to analyze brain MRI for dementia, Alzheimer disease progression, and neuropathology prediction. A recurring theme is improving model interpretability and transportability across populations and cohorts.

topics.title: White Matter Change and Mobility Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on age-related white matter microstructural changes across major tracts and their links to motor and mobility outcomes in older adults. Studies use longitudinal diffusion MRI approaches to track corpus callosum and related pathways, including associations with proprioception, multidomain mobility, and risk states such as REM sleep behavior disorder.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
predictive modeling for reliable cognitive outcomes
topics.title: Multimodal Connectome Prediction of Cognitive Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting brain and cognitive outcomes from multimodal connectome and neuroimaging data, especially in youth and memory-related settings. It emphasizes integrated brain connectivity features and predictive modeling for reliable cognitive performance and related phenotypes.

keywords: pediatric adult automated mri segmentation tools
domain randomization for post operative temporal resection analysis
topics.title: Generalizable Pediatric MRI Segmentation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on robust and generalizable MRI segmentation approaches for pediatric, neonatal, and related brain structures. It emphasizes transfer learning, domain randomization, and validation for clinical and longitudinal applications.

topics.title: White Matter Hyperintensity Microstructure and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities and related white matter integrity changes, especially microstructural signatures measured with diffusion metrics. It emphasizes clinical correlates, including cognitive impairment and small vessel disease.

topics.title: Vascular and Aging Influences on Brain Health
topics.description: This cluster centers on how vascular risk, peripheral vascular health, and aging relate to brain structure, white matter integrity, and cognition. It also includes links to dementia risk, inflammation, and brain clearance systems in large cohort studies.

keywords: robust ensemble learning for practical ischemic stroke lesion segmentation
topics.title: Robust Brain MRI Segmentation and Detection
topics.description: This cluster focuses on robust and generalizable brain MRI analysis methods for lesion segmentation, anomaly detection, and related clinical tasks. It emphasizes ensemble, self-supervised, multimodal, and domain adaptation approaches evaluated for clinical robustness and practical downstream use.

keywords: deep learning denoise for single excitation wideband
microstructure preserving diffusion mri super resolution
topics.title: Deep Learning for Accelerated MRI Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning and generative approaches for accelerated MRI reconstruction, denoising, super-resolution, and related optimization tasks. It also includes multimodal synthesis and harmonization methods for improving neuroimaging analysis across sites and sequences.

keywords: normative models of functional spreading network
topics.title: Normative EEG Models in Dementia Progression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on normative modeling approaches for EEG and functional connectivity to capture heterogeneity and abnormal activity beyond group averages. The studies target dementia-related disorders such as Alzheimer disease, MCI, and Lewy body disease, with emphasis on progression and spatiotemporal variability.

keywords: select functional connectivity feature for prediction of phenotypes
genetic foundations of environmental sensitivity indexed
topics.title: Functional Connectivity Predicts Individual Differences
topics.description: This cluster centers on using functional and structural connectome features to predict individual differences in cognition, intelligence, and phenotypes. It also highlights individualized brain organization, dynamic brain states, and connectome-based feature selection approaches.

topics.title: Precision Mapping of Individual Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying and characterizing individual-specific brain network organization using resting-state functional connectivity and precision mapping approaches. It also emphasizes reliability, reproducibility, and how factors like scan length and cohort size affect connectome and fingerprinting analyses.

topics.title: Alzheimer continuum, amyloid, and tau
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease across the preclinical to clinical continuum, emphasizing amyloid and tau pathology, cognitive decline, and risk factors such as APOE4. It also reflects work on predicting disease progression and resilience in aging and preclinical stages.

keywords: automatic mask free lesion aware structural conncectivity estimation
mask free lesion aware structural conncectivity estimation
topics.title: MRI Markers of Multiple Sclerosis Damage
topics.description: The cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of multiple sclerosis, especially white matter lesions, disconnection, and neurodegenerative change linked to disability and cognitive status. It also includes diffusion MRI and lesion-aware connectivity methods for assessing disease severity and early MS.

topics.title: Normative Modeling and Disease Subtyping
topics.description: This cluster centers on using neuroanatomical and MRI-based modeling to identify biomarkers, predict cognitive decline, and classify disease subtypes and stages. The work spans dementia-related phenotyping, individualized risk mapping, and machine learning approaches for diagnosis and staging.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Degeneration and Brainstem Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and molecular MRI correlates of Parkinson disease, especially early degeneration in the substantia nigra and related brainstem nuclei. It also links iron and neuromelanin changes with motor symptoms and REM sleep behavior disorder.

topics.title: Subtyping Neurodegenerative Progression with MRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying subtypes and progression patterns in neurodegenerative disease, especially Parkinson disease, using MRI-based biomarkers and related clinical measures. Methods such as machine learning and deep learning are used to model spatiotemporal trajectories, staging, and degeneration of key brain regions.
